<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/install/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/install/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

# Maestro fleet status line

Registers a Claude Code `statusLine` that renders the live state of every maestro
orchestration topic — agent-free. The conductor already updates its session
manifests every tick; the status line just reads them, so it stays current with
zero agent involvement (Claude re-runs it on `refreshInterval`).

Line format (one segment per topic that still has active/pending work; fully-done
topics disappear):

```
🎼 <topic> <done>/<total>✓ ▶<active>(<ids>) ⏳<pending> ✅<pr-ready> ⚠<pr-broken>
```

It registers through the **shared statusline host** (`~/.claude/statusline-host.sh`),
which owns Claude Code's single statusLine slot at a fixed, checkout-independent
path and renders every registered bar (maestro 🎼, work ⚙, follow-up 🔄, qc 🔬).
Installing only drops the maestro **fragment** — it never clobbers the other bars,
and because the slot points at the fixed host, every new tab/session picks it up
with no reinstall.

## Commands

- **Install:** `node "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills/install/scripts/install-statusline.js"`
- **Show resolved paths + current config:** `... install-statusline.js --print`
- **Remove (drop just the maestro bar):** `... install-statusline.js --remove`

After installing, run `/reload-plugins` (or just wait for the next refresh tick).

## How it fits together

- **Host:** `~/.claude/statusline-host.sh` — the single registered statusLine; renders
  every fragment under `~/.claude/statuslines/*.cmd` in filename order.
- **Fragment:** `~/.claude/statuslines/10-maestro.cmd` — one line, the path to this
  plugin's renderer. Owned solely by the maestro installer.
- **Renderer:** `skills/lib/maestro-statusline.sh` → calls `maestro-statusline.js`.
- **Data source:** `~/.cache/maestro/sessions/*.json` (manifests) + `/tmp/maestro-alerts.jsonl`.
